summary_one_line: Input must form a valid HTTP response with a parseable Set-Cookie header so a cookie is added, making the freelist loop iterate

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The input must contain the exact literal tokens that the cookie path requires: an HTTP status line ("HTTP/" at offset 0x24) and a "Set-Cookie" header ("Set-Coo" at offset 0x39+), plus a well-formed host:port so the response is parsed far enough to call Curl_cookie_add. Only then does a Cookie node exist, so Curl_cookie_freelist's while(co) takes the TRUE side at least once.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All winner seeds carry the intact literals at fixed offsets: 0x24 is uniformly 'G' followed by "HTTP/" (47 48 54 54 50 2f), and 0x39 onward holds "Set-Coo". The HIT-COUNT DIVERGENCE shows Curl_cookie_add fires 20x in W and 0x in L, and the blocker takes T=20 in W vs T=0 in L, so a cookie is added and freed.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds are short and byte-noise: at offset 0x24 W is uniformly 'G' but L is a scattered set (50/42/9e/2f), and the entire "HTTP/".."Set-Coo" region is dominated by '%' filler in L, so the response never parses a Set-Cookie header; Curl_cookie_add never runs and the freelist while(co) stays on the F side (T=0).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the program's expected literal ("HTTP/", "Set-Cookie") into the input at the matching offsets, planting the exact magic bytes that the HTTP/cookie parser strcmp/memcmp against. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace, so they must stumble onto these multi-byte literals by chance and never do within budget.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the loop guard while(co) in Curl_cookie_freelist whose TRUE side is globally blocked.
Both decisive pairs differ only by the I2S technique with full prob_div=1.00.
The winner takes the loop TRUE side 20 times while the loser never does.
Curl_cookie_add executes 20 times under the winner seed set and 0 times under the loser set.
Winner seeds carry the literal "HTTP/" at offset 0x24 onward.
Winner seeds carry the "Set-Coo" cookie-header literal in the tail bytes.
At offset 0x24 winners are uniformly 'G' (x20) while losers are a scattered noisy set.
Loser seeds are short and lack the HTTP/Set-Cookie structure (e.g. 32-byte byte-noise seed).
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which scans the input for an operand and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so it is a clean I2S-negative arm.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 58 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 2b428b2993f47feb` and observed two I2S-floor rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 14 and 15 of the 16-level ancestor chain; the trailing 'I2S-floor signal' line reports 2 dash rows, direct lower-bound evidence of an I2SRandReplace find in the winner's lineage.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where naive/value_profile resolve the while(co) TRUE side at the same rate as cmplog/vpc despite the input still needing the exact multi-byte "HTTP/" and "Set-Cookie" literals — showing the literals are reachable by havoc alone and I2S substitution is not the discriminator.

weakest_evidence_point: The chain from 'cookie added' to the freelist loop is inferred from per-function hit counts (Curl_cookie_add 20 vs 0); no seed directly proves the parsed Set-Cookie produced a non-NULL c->cookies[i] entry rather than another path keeping co non-NULL.
